title: POSIX functions should not be called with arguments that trigger buffer overflows
description: When the size argument exceeds the actual size of the buffer, the memory management functions will either read or write data past the buffer memory boundary into adjacent memory. Because the memory might be uninitialized or contain other pieces of data, the behavior of the affected function can not be predicted and will likely trigger bugs. What is the potential impact? Buffer boundaries violations can lead to serious security issues.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
char buffer[10];
memcpy(buffer, source, 20); // Noncompliant: copying 20 bytes into 10-byte buffer

char dest[5];
strcpy(dest, "This is a very long string"); // Noncompliant: source too long for dest

int array[5];
fread(array, sizeof(int), 10, file); // Noncompliant: reading 10 elements into 5-element array
compliant:
char buffer[20];
memcpy(buffer, source, 10); // Compliant: buffer is large enough

char dest[50];
strcpy(dest, "Short string"); // Compliant: dest is large enough

int array[10];
fread(array, sizeof(int), 10, file); // Compliant: array size matches read count

checks.message: POSIX functions should not be called with arguments that trigger buffer overflows. Buffer size mismatch detected.
